pages.text:
Dr. Isabella Rossi
Rome, Italy
Distinguished Professor of Art History with over 35 years of scholarly excellence specializing in 
Renaissance and Baroque art, with particular focus on Italian painting and patronage networks. 
Internationally recognized authority on Caravaggio and the cultural exchanges between Italy and 
Northern Europe in the 16th-17th centuries. Author of 8 books and over 160 scholarly articles. 
Recipient of numerous prestigious awards including Guggenheim Fellowship and membership in 
multiple national academies. Extensive curatorial experience including major exhibitions at the 
Uffizi, Louvre, and Metropolitan Museum of Art.
ACADEMIC APPOINTMENTS
Professor Emerita of Art History
Sapienza University of Rome, Department of History, Anthropology, Religions, Art and Performing 
Arts | Italy | 2020 – Present
Full Professor of Art History
Sapienza University of Rome | 1998-2020
Associate Professor
University of Bologna, Department of the Arts | Italy | 1991–1998
Assistant Professor
University of Florence | Italy | 1986-1991
Lecturer
University of Siena | Italy | 1982–1986
Visiting Professorships:

Getty Research Institute, Los Angeles (2015-2016, full year)

Princeton University, Department of Art and Archaeology (Spring 2010)

Yale University, Department of the History of Art (Fall 2004)

The Courtauld Institute of Art, University of London (1999-2000)

Harvard University, Department of History of Art and Architecture (Spring 1995)
EDUCATION
Dottorato di Ricerca (PhD) in Art History
Scuola Normale Superiore di Pisa | Italy | 1978-1982

Dissertation: "Patronage and Power: The Medici Court and Artistic Production in Late 16th Century 
Florence"
Supervisor: Prof. Roberto Longhi (posthumously), Prof. Mina Gregori
Laurea in Lettere (MA equivalent)
Università degli Studi di Roma "La Sapienza" | Italy | 1973-1978
Summa cum laude
Thesis on Caravaggio's early Roman period
Liceo Classico Diploma
Liceo Classico Torquato Tasso, Rome | 1968-1973
RESEARCH AREAS
Italian Renaissance and Baroque painting (15th-17th centuries); Caravaggio and Caravaggism; 
Artistic patronage and collecting practices; Cultural exchange between Italy and Northern Europe; 
Gender and art in early modern Italy; Conservation and technical art history; Reception history and 
historiography of Italian art; Sacred and profane imagery in Counter-Reformation Rome
MONOGRAPHS & BOOKS
1. Rossi, I. (2023). *Caravaggio's Legacy: Influence and Innovation in European Baroque Painting*. 
Yale University Press, 680 pp.
2. Rossi, I. (2019). *Women Patrons and Collectors in Renaissance Italy*. Cambridge University 
Press, 425 pp.
3. Rossi, I. & Schmidt, H. (eds.) (2016). *Beyond Borders: Artistic Exchange between Italy and the 
Low Countries, 1500-1700*. Brill, 512 pp.
4. Rossi, I. (2012). *Caravaggio: The Complete Works*. Phaidon Press, 800 pp. [Translated into 12 
languages]
5. Rossi, I. (2008). *Sacred and Profane Love: Art and Devotion in Counter-Reformation Rome*. 
University of Chicago Press, 356 pp.
6. Rossi, I., Martinez, A., & Chen, L. (2004). *The Medici Collections: Art, Power and Identity*. Electa, 
445 pp.
7. Rossi, I. (1997). *Artemisia Gentileschi and the Authority of Art*. Pennsylvania State University 
Press, 298 pp.
8. Rossi, I. (1989). *Light and Shadow: Caravaggio's Roman Period, 1592-1606*. Princeton 
University Press, 412 pp.

SELECTED JOURNAL ARTICLES (FROM 160+ TOTAL)
1. Rossi, I. (2024). "New Attributions and Technical Evidence: Reassessing Caravaggio's Workshop 
Practices." *The Burlington Magazine*, 166(1450), 12-29.
2. Rossi, I. & Li, W. (2023). "Transnational Patronage Networks in Early Modern Europe: New 
Documentary Evidence." *Art Bulletin*, 105(3), 45-68.
3. Rossi, I. (2022). "Gender, Agency, and Artistic Production in Seventeenth-Century Rome." 
*Renaissance Quarterly*, 75(4), 1245-1289.
4. Martinez, A. & Rossi, I. (2022). "Caravaggio's Judith Beheading Holofernes: New Technical 
Analysis and Conservation Findings." *Studies in Conservation*, 67(5), 334-351.
5. Rossi, I. (2021). "The Cardinal's Collection: Francesco Maria del Monte and the Formation of 
Baroque Taste." *Journal of the History of Collections*, 33(2), 189-214.
6. Rossi, I. & Johnson, R. (2020). "Rethinking the Sacred-Profane Divide in Counter-Reformation 
Art." *Art History*, 43(4), 678-705.
7. Rossi, I. (2019). "Material Culture and Devotional Practice in Early Modern Italy." *Renaissance 
Studies*, 33(5), 722-747.
8. Rossi, I. (2018). "Caravaggio's Narcissus: Iconography, Patronage, and Meaning." *Artibus et 
Historiae*, 39(77), 145-168.
9. Chen, L., Rossi, I., & Schmidt, H. (2017). "Digital Humanities and Art History: New Methodologies 
for Studying Patronage Networks." *International Journal for Digital Art History*, 3, 67-89.
10. Rossi, I. (2016). "The Contarelli Chapel Commission: New Archival Documents." *Mitteilungen 
des Kunsthistorischen Institutes in Florenz*, 58(2), 156-184.
11. Rossi, I. (2015). "Artemisia Gentileschi Reconsidered: Agency, Authorship, and Recent 
Scholarship." *Woman's Art Journal*, 36(1), 3-18.
12. Rossi, I. & Martinez, A. (2013). "Technical Analysis and the Attribution Problem: Case Studies 
from Caravaggio's Circle." *Metropolitan Museum Journal*, 48, 127-152.
13. Rossi, I. (2011). "Collecting Caravaggio: The Formation of Early Modern Taste." *Journal of the 
Warburg and Courtauld Institutes*, 74, 243-271.
14. Rossi, I. (2009). "The Artist as Theologian: Religious Iconography in Caravaggio's Late Works." 
*Zeitschrift für Kunstgeschichte*, 72(3), 387-412.
15. Rossi, I. (2006). "Cultural Exchange and Artistic Innovation: Italian Artists in the Netherlands." 
*Simiolus*, 32(4), 234-259.
EXHIBITION CATALOGUES


Rossi, I. (ed.) (2021). *Caravaggio and His Followers in Rome*. Exhibition catalogue, Galleria 
Borghese/Louvre. Officina Libraria, 456 pp.

Rossi, I. & Schmidt, H. (2018). *The Age of Caravaggio*. Exhibition catalogue, Metropolitan 
Museum of Art. Yale University Press, 380 pp.

Rossi, I. (2015). *Artemisia Gentileschi: Power and Passion*. Exhibition catalogue, Palazzo 
Braschi, Rome. Skira, 320 pp.

Multiple catalogue essays for major exhibitions at Uffizi Gallery, National Gallery London, Prado 
Museum (1990-2024)
BOOK CHAPTERS & ENCYCLOPEDIA ENTRIES
Over 80 contributed chapters and encyclopedia entries including contributions to Grove Art Online,
Oxford Art Online, and major edited volumes on Renaissance and Baroque art.

TEACHING


Italian Renaissance Art (undergraduate survey, 150-200 students)



Caravaggio and Caravaggism (graduate seminar, 15-20 students)

Methods and Theories in Art History (graduate, 20-25 students)

Dissertation Seminars for PhD students

Museum Studies Practicum (graduate)
University of Bologna (1991-1998)

History of Italian Painting 1400-1600

Women Artists in Early Modern Europe

Patronage and Collecting Practices
Earlier Positions: Taught courses in Italian Renaissance and Baroque art, iconography, 
historiography
GRADUATE SUPERVISION
Doctoral Students - Completed: 34
Selected recent completions:







Dr. Elena Bianchi (2012-2018): "Sacred Art in Counter-Reformation" - Now Associate Professor,
University of Padua

Dr. James Foster (2010-2016): "Caravaggio's Technique and Materials" - Now Conservation 
Scientist, Getty Conservation Institute
Current PhD Students: 3
MA/Laurea Magistrale Students: Supervised over 120 thesis projects (1986-2020)



PEER REVIEW & MANUSCRIPT EVALUATION
Extensive reviewing for major publishers (Yale, Princeton, Cambridge, Chicago, Oxford University 
Presses) and journals. Over 400 manuscript reviews. External examiner for PhD theses at 25+ 
universities internationally.
CONFERENCE PRESENTATIONS & KEYNOTES


Getty Research Institute Symposium (2019)

Clark Art Institute Lecture Series (2018)

National Gallery of Art, Washington - A. W. Mellon Lectures (2014)
MEDIA APPEARANCES & PUBLIC ENGAGEMENT


Consultant and on-screen expert for BBC documentary series *The Art of Italy* (2023)


TEDx talk on Caravaggio and modern visual culture (2019, 1.2M views)
